response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience (JS/TS, DLang, Node.js, some Java/C++) and strong craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools). However, the role demands deep JVM expertise, Kotlin proficiency, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, and a genuine interest in IDE internals — none of which are evidenced in the resume. Java appears only as a listed skill with minimal context (robotic arms, mobile orchestration), there is no Kotlin or Spring exposure, and IDE-internals experience is limited to brief C/C++ work on LabWindows/CVI a decade ago. Strong engineer, wrong stack.
strengths: Proven track record on complex, large-scale systems (Sociomantic low-latency micro-services, GIS platform)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, authored own test framework
Some IDE-adjacent experience writing C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin experience and only superficial Java exposure
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate ecosystem experience mentioned
No evidence of IDE-internals or language-tooling work beyond a brief NI stint a decade ago

key_factors.factor: Lack of Spring/JVM enterprise framework experience
Strong engineering craftsmanship and complex-systems track record
